root_text.an3.82:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gadrabho gogaṇaṁ piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandho hoti:
root_text.an3.82:1.3: Tassa na tādiso vaṇṇo hoti seyyathāpi gunnaṁ, na tādiso saro hoti seyyathāpi gunnaṁ, na tādisaṁ padaṁ hoti seyyathāpi gunnaṁ.
root_text.an3.82:1.4: So gogaṇaṁyeva piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandho hoti:
root_text.an3.82:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco bhikkhu bhikkhusaṅghaṁ piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandho hoti:
root_text.an3.82:2.3: Tassa na tādiso chando hoti adhisīlasikkhāsamādāne seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ, na tādiso chando hoti adhicittasikkhāsamādāne seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ, na tādiso chando hoti adhipaññāsikkhāsamādāne seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ.
root_text.an3.82:2.4: So bhikkhusaṅghaṁyeva piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandho hoti:
root_text.an3.82:3.2: ‘tibbo no chando bhavissati adhisīlasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo no chando bhavissati adhicittasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo no chando bhavissati adhipaññāsikkhāsamādāne’ti.

translation_text.an3.82:1.1: “Suppose, mendicants, a donkey followed behind a herd of cattle, thinking:
translation_text.an3.82:1.2: ‘I can moo too! I can moo too!’
translation_text.an3.82:1.3: But it doesn’t look like a cow, or sound like a cow, or leave a footprint like a cow.
translation_text.an3.82:1.4: Still it follows behind a herd of cattle, thinking:
translation_text.an3.82:1.5: ‘I can moo too! I can moo too!’
translation_text.an3.82:2.1: In the same way, some mendicant follows behind the mendicant Saṅgha, thinking:
translation_text.an3.82:2.2: ‘I’m a monk too! I’m a monk too!’
translation_text.an3.82:2.3: But they don’t have the same enthusiasm for undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom as the other mendicants.
translation_text.an3.82:2.4: Still they follow behind the mendicant Saṇgha, thinking:
translation_text.an3.82:2.5: ‘I’m a monk too! I’m a monk too!’
translation_text.an3.82:3.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.82:3.2: ‘We will have keen enthusiasm for undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.’

variant_text.an3.82:1.2: ahampi dammo, ahampi dammo’ti → ahampamhā ahampamhāti (bj); ahampi go amhā ahampi go amhāti (si); ahampi amhā ahampi amhāti (sya-all, km, pts1ed); ahampi go ahampi goti (?)
